name: South of the Border Jalapeno Cream Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Jalapeno peppers; seeded and finely minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Butter an 8-inch round cake pan. Place a waxed paper circle on the bottom, butter it, and set aside.
3. In the top of a double boiler over simmering water, melt chocolate with the liqueur. Stir until smooth. Set aside to cool.
4. Cream together the butter and sugar in a large bowl using an electric mixer on medium speed until thick and pale.
5. Add yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition until well blended.
6. Turn the mixer speed to low and mix in the chocolate mixture until well combined. Stir in vanilla extract.
7. Add flour, cinnamon, almonds, and jalapeno peppers; beat until well combined.
8. In another bowl, beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff peaks form.
9. With a rubber spatula, fold 1/3 of the egg whites into the chocolate batter until well mixed.
10. Fold in the remaining egg whites until no streaks of white are visible.
11. Pour batter into prepared pan and place on the center rack of the oven. Bake for 25 minutes.
12. Let cool in pan on a wire rack to room temperature for at least half an hour.
13. Run a thin, sharp knife around the edge of the cake, invert onto a plate, and peel off waxed paper.
14. Serve plain or dust with powdered sugar.
